frameworks.name: context-dependent evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Context gates selection and accumulation of sensory evidence for decisions

frameworks.name: dynamical systems analysis of decision networks
frameworks.one_line: Three dynamical solutions can support context-dependent computation

frameworks.name: individual variability of neural computation
frameworks.one_line: Different subjects implement different mixtures of the same solutions

core_question: How do individual brains implement context-dependent selection and accumulation of evidence, and how does this implementation vary across subjects?
key_assumptions: Assumptions supported by both monkey and rat data constrain the dynamical analysis
Context-dependent computation can be characterized by a small set of dynamical solutions
Behavioural performance can be uniformly good despite heterogeneous neural mechanisms
manipulations: Designed a novel context-cued evidence-accumulation task in rats
Trained many rats via automated high-throughput methods to sample variability
Compared neural and behavioural signatures across individual subjects
key_findings: Mathematically identified three dynamical solutions that can support the task
Existing electrophysiology and modelling data are compatible with all combinations of these solutions
Substantial neural and behavioural heterogeneity across rats despite uniformly good performance
A predicted link between behavioural and neural signatures was robustly supported
interpretations: Different individuals may use different combinations of the same dynamical solutions
Individual variability is a structured feature, not noise, of context-dependent computation
